discovery.run_summary: Strong risk-on tape with VIX collapsing on news of a potential Iran deal 'subject to finalization.' Semis leading (SMH +6.75%), defense rallying despite (or because of) de-escalation framing, oil down on supply-return expectations. ORCL down hard (-8.5%) is the conspicuous mega-cap outlier against the broad rally.

notable_index_moves: SMH +6.75% leading; semis broadly bid (ONTO, AEIS, VECO, LSCC all up 8-13%)
ITA +4.97% — defense rallying despite Iran de-escalation, suggests geopolitical premium not unwinding; KTOS, AVAV, MRCY, DCO all up
VIX -12.5% to 19.44 confirms risk-on regime shift
XLE -1.94% — oil lower on prospect of restored Iranian/Hormuz supply
ORCL -8.5% on heavy volume — idiosyncratic, against tape
IWM +2.96% — small/mid caps participating broadly, lifting many movers on beta alone

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semicap name leading on sector-wide SMH surge
discoveries.thesis: Move is consistent with SMH +6.75% on a risk-on, semis-leadership day. ONTO is a high-beta semicap that routinely amplifies SMH moves ~2x. Volume only at average (1.03x) argues against a name-specific catalyst — this looks like beta + short cover in a thin float on a melt-up day, not idiosyncratic news.
discoveries.what_confirms: No 8-K appears in coming days; peers (KLAC, LRCX, AMAT) confirm sympathy magnitude proportional to SMH.
discoveries.what_kills: An 8-K or news item surfaces showing a contract win, guidance raise, or analyst upgrade specific to ONTO that would re-cast this as underdone.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Average-volume large-percentage moves on broad sector rally days usually decompose to beta, not catalyst — check volume_multiple before assuming idiosyncratic story.
discoveries.catalyst: Sector-wide semicap rally; no company-specific catalyst visible
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty {} and volume_multiple is 1.03 — no SEC filings, no earnings, no volume signature of a news event.
research_pointers: Compare ONTO's move to KLAC/LRCX/AMAT to confirm pure beta
Check for any HBM/advanced packaging order flow news on the wire
Watch for follow-up filings in next 48 hours

discoveries.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Airline rallying on oil collapse plus risk-on tape
discoveries.thesis: Iran deal framing → oil down ~2% → airline fuel cost relief, layered on risk-on tape and IWM strength. ALK at ~$5B cap is a smaller airline with high fuel sensitivity. If the Iran deal actually finalizes in 'next few days' as Trump claims, oil leg-down extends and ALK's fuel cost tailwind compounds. The move is large but airline operating leverage to fuel is enormous — could underextend if crude breaks lower.
discoveries.what_confirms: Crude continues lower into the week; SKYW and other small airlines confirm sector follow-through; no idiosyncratic ALK news emerges (move is clean macro).
discoveries.what_kills: Iran deal fails to materialize and oil reverses; or an ALK-specific 8-K surfaces showing a labor cost or operational issue that re-frames this as a relief bounce off a prior decline.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Geopolitical-shock reversals create asymmetric setups in fuel-sensitive industrials — airlines and trucking often underprice the duration of relief in the first 24-48 hours of news.
discoveries.catalyst: Trump's claim of Iran deal 'subject to finalization' → oil lower → airline fuel relief
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: XLE -1.94% confirms oil response; both ALK and SKYW up materially; no company-specific 8-K means this is the macro catalyst.
research_pointers: Check ALK fuel hedge ratio for next 2 quarters
Track Brent/WTI move into Asian session for follow-through
Compare ALK move to LUV, DAL, UAL for relative magnitude

discoveries.name: Kratos Defense & Security Solutions
discoveries.setup: Defense small-cap rallying into Iran de-escalation narrative
discoveries.thesis: KTOS up 7% but five_day_change is -7.3% — this looks like a bounce off a defense pullback rather than fresh strength. The puzzling part: if Iran is settling, the geopolitical premium that drove defense names higher should unwind, not extend. ITA +5% suggests the market is reading 'Trump shows force → deals get done → more defense spend, not less,' but that read may not hold once the deal language firms up. KTOS is high-beta defense; the move likely overshoots and fades when the de-escalation reality sinks in.
discoveries.what_confirms: Iran deal actually finalizes in next few days, removing immediate-conflict premium; ITA fades from highs; KTOS gives back this move within a week.
discoveries.what_kills: Deal falls through and conflict escalates, in which case KTOS extends. Or DoD budget news / specific Kratos contract award surfaces that justifies the move on fundamentals not geopolitics.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Defense names rallying on de-escalation news is a recurring pattern that often reverses — the first instinct ('strength = more defense spend') is frequently sold once peace-deal language firms up.
discoveries.catalyst: Defense sympathy on Iran headlines + IWM strength
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals, volume_multiple 0.82 (below average), and 5-day still negative — pattern looks like a bounce/beta move not a news-driven gap.
research_pointers: Track ITA into next 2 sessions for de-escalation fade
Watch for actual Iran deal text/signing event as the catalyst trigger
Compare KTOS move to AVAV and LMT for selective vs broad defense bid

discoveries.name: Builders FirstSource, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Building products rallying with 5.02 officer-change 8-K filed days earlier
discoveries.thesis: BLDR has a recent 5.02 (officer/director departure or appointment) filing from 2026-06-05 — six days before today's move. The move is occurring on below-average volume (0.69x) into a strong risk-on tape with rate-sensitive names bid. The 5.02 by itself doesn't tell us whether the change was bullish (new CEO, market liked it) or bearish (CFO departure, would weigh on stock). Hard to commit a direction without reading the filing text.
discoveries.what_confirms: Reading the 8-K exhibit reveals which scenario this is — if it's a planned appointment received well, today's move extends; if it's an abrupt departure with a delayed sell reaction, this rally is a bounce that fades.
discoveries.what_kills: Today's move is purely beta to IWM/housing tape and the 5.02 is irrelevant noise.
discoveries.catalyst: Risk-on tape + housing/builder bid; older 5.02 filing context
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: 5.02 filing from 6/5 is the only company-specific signal; today's move is on below-average volume, suggesting macro not the filing.
research_pointers: READ the 8-K exhibit — determine who left/joined and whether planned
Compare BLDR move to other homebuilder/building product names today
Check if 5.02 was a CEO or CFO event — different implications

discoveries.name: Mercury Systems Inc
discoveries.setup: Defense small-cap gap up with no 8-K, only ~1% five-day net change
discoveries.thesis: MRCY up nearly 12% on 1.25x volume with no SEC filing and no earnings catalyst — yet 5-day change is barely positive. That pattern says the move is concentrated entirely in today's session and likely driven by a short squeeze or sympathy with ITA strength, not fundamentals. Defense small-caps with high short interest often spike sharply on risk-on days then fade. Without a confirming catalyst this looks like a momentum/squeeze move that overshoots.
discoveries.what_confirms: ITA fades over next 2 sessions and MRCY gives back disproportionately; no contract or guidance news emerges in coming days.
discoveries.what_kills: A Mercury-specific contract win or analyst upgrade surfaces; or short interest data shows the move was structurally supported by genuine new buyers, not squeeze mechanics.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Single-day defense spikes with flat five-day context and no 8-K are a recurring squeeze pattern — worth fading on a 1-3 day horizon if the broader defense tape rolls.
discoveries.catalyst: Defense sympathy + risk-on tape; no company-specific catalyst
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals plus near-zero 5-day change despite +12% today strongly suggests squeeze/momentum, not news.
research_pointers: Check MRCY short interest and days-to-cover
Look for any defense contract announcement on DoD daily wire
Watch tomorrow's open — squeeze moves often gap down

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Trump claims Iran deal 'subject to finalization' → crude lower

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump claims Iran defenses 'GONE' and signals intent to take Kharg Island and 'assume total control' of Iran's oil and gas markets, compared to Venezuela playbook.
trump_signals.note: Post is escalatory in tone but later same-day reporting frames a deal 'subject to finalization' — the market is reading this as resolution-positive (VIX -12.5%, oil down). The Kharg Island / Venezuela framing is unusual and worth tracking: if taken literally, US assuming oil market 'control' is supply-bearish for crude (more supply restored faster) and bullish for airlines/transports. Iranian state media threatening Musk's companies (per separate CNBC report) is a counter-signal that the situation is not settled.

ai_announcements.summary: SpaceX completes pricing for its public debut; tangential AI angle via Starlink/xAI ecosystem and Grok safety lawsuit overhang.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Kratos benefits from defense/space sector enthusiasm around SpaceX IPO; unmanned/space systems peer rally. Not a direct AI announcement impact.
research_pointers: Check if KTOS/AVAV/MRCY move is broader defense-space sympathy with SpaceX IPO day
Look for any DoD contract news same day

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Sympathy bounce with SpaceX IPO + defense/aerospace AI autonomy theme. AVAV was down 10% over 5 days, so this is partial mean reversion.
research_pointers: Confirm whether move is IPO-day sector lift vs idiosyncratic news
Low volume multiple (0.52x) suggests not heavy conviction buying

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic's Dario Amodei has just one direct report
ai_announcements.summary: Profile piece on Anthropic org structure. No product, capability, or competitive announcement.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Today's AI news flow is light on stock-moving catalysts. Biggest movers (ONTO +12.7%, ALK +11.95%, MRCY +11.7%, AEIS +10.5%, LSCC +7.9%, VECO +8.5%, EXTR +8.7%) appear driven by non-AI catalysts not present in the provided news (likely M&A, sector rotation, or SpaceX-IPO-day risk-on in semicaps/defense/airlines). Semicap equipment cluster (ONTO/VECO/AEIS/LSCC) moving together suggests a specific semicap catalyst (possibly an analyst upgrade, AI capex datapoint, or M&A rumor) outside this news set — worth investigating separately. ALK +12% on no AI news is almost certainly airline-specific (fuel, guidance, or M&A). BLDR has an 8-K officer change as the likely driver. No clear AI-announcement-driven irrational selloffs or underdone rallies in today's data.
